title: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk to be released as govt revokes NSA detention
content: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk's detention under the National Security Act has been revoked by the Centre with immediate effect. Wangchuk was detained over allegations of inciting unrest in Ladakh, with the government citing his remarks as potentially destabilizing. His wife had challenged the detention in the Supreme Court.

title: Explosion damages Jewish school in Amsterdam; mayor calls it targeted attack
content: An explosion rocked a Jewish school in Amsterdam, with the mayor suspecting a targeted attack on the community. This follows a similar incident at a Rotterdam synagogue, where arson is suspected. Authorities detained teenagers near another synagogue. An Islamist group has claimed responsibility for both Dutch incidents and a prior attack in Belgium, raising concerns.

title: 'He is riding his luck': Uthappa's big comment on Sooryavanshi ahead of IPL 2026
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, after a stellar IPL debut, faces heightened expectations and increased scrutiny in his second season. Former India cricketer Robin Uthappa suggests this year will be a crucial learning phase as opponents have analyzed his game. While expecting him to score, Uthappa believes the element of surprise has faded, making this season pivotal for his long-term development.

title: The great heist trail: Robberies grow bolder and more organised with guns, knives and deception
content: Despite the digital age, India witnesses daring real-life heists targeting large cash movements and businesses. From Delhi's Chandni Chowk to Surat's highways and Karnataka's banks, armed gangs employ deception, intimidation, and violence in carefully planned robberies, highlighting a persistent challenge for law enforcement.

title: Meta is planning biggest-ever layoffs: Here's why 15,000 jobs could be at risk
content: Meta is  planning its largest-ever layoffs, potentially impacting over 20% of its workforce that equals to about 15,000-plus employees. This significant move is driven by soaring AI infrastructure costs and a strategic shift towards leaner, AI-assisted operations. Executives are already mapping out reductions, as the company invests billions in AI development and acquisitions, while facing internal challenges with its AI models.

title: 'Threatened to kill me': Delhi woman 'molested' during Uber bike ride
content: An Uber bike rider allegedly molested a woman during a late-night ride in Delhi. The driver reportedly behaved inappropriately, sped up the bike, and threatened her when she objected. He eventually dropped her off and fled after she threatened to jump.

title: What is DME? Scientists look to scale up output to offset LPG supply blow
content: India is exploring increased production of dimethyl ether (DME) as an alternative to LPG amid global oil market disruptions. A government research institute has established a pilot plant and plans to scale up DME output, a fuel scientists say can be used with existing LPG infrastructure, addressing supply challenges and rising prices.

title: Why Calcutta HC allowed kidney transplants from unrelated donors in 2 renal failure cases
content: The Calcutta High Court has directed the health department's authorization committee to permit kidney transplants for two renal failure patients from unrelated donors. The court intervened after the committee's delayed and seemingly flimsy rejections, emphasizing the need for expedited decisions in urgent transplant cases as per law.

title: Accenture CEO Julie Sweet to company’s employees: If you want a promotion, you need to ...
content: Accenture CEO Julie Sweet has mandated that employees must demonstrate proficiency in using AI tools to be considered for promotions. This requirement reflects the consultancy's integration of AI into its core operations, a shift Sweet likens to the adoption of computers in the past. The company has invested significantly in reskilling its workforce to adapt to these evolving workplace technologies.

title: 'Wasn't saying nice things': Salman Ali Agha on war of words Mehidy Hasan Miraz
content: A controversial run-out of Pakistan's Salman Ali Agha by Bangladesh captain Mehidy Hasan Miraz reignited the 'spirit of cricket' debate. Agha, stepping out of his crease, bent to retrieve the ball, only for Miraz to swiftly dislodge the bails. While Agha felt he would have acted differently, he acknowledged the dismissal was within the rules, calling it a 'heat-of-the-moment' incident.

title: LPG shortage: Air fryer, rice cooker and 6 other appliances you can use to make food
content: India's LPG shortage, fueled by Middle East conflict, is driving a surge in demand for induction cooktops and other electric cooking appliances. As gas stoves become scarce, households are exploring alternatives like air fryers, microwaves, rice cookers, and electric pans to prepare everyday meals. These devices offer solutions for basic cooking needs during supply disruptions.

title: Deepika Padukone says Mumbai is ‘choking’ - how mayor responded
content: Deepika Padukone's social media post highlighting Mumbai's poor air quality prompted a response from Mayor Ritu Tawde. The civic body acknowledged rising AQI levels and stated that measures are being implemented, including dust control at construction sites and issuing stop-work notices for non-compliance. Experts attribute the recent low visibility to smog.

title: Watch: Iran strikes US embassy in Baghdad, smoke rises over compound
content: Tensions flared in the Middle East as Iran reportedly targeted the US embassy in Baghdad with drones following a stern warning. The attack, which saw thick black smoke rising from the compound, occurred shortly after strikes hit powerful Iran-backed group Kataeb Hezbollah. Iraq finds itself increasingly drawn into the regional conflict.

title: Meet Sadanand Date: MBBS-IPS officer and President’s awardee who is the new SEBI executive director
content: Sadanand Date, a seasoned IPS officer with extensive experience in the CBI and Mumbai Police's economic offenses units, has been appointed as SEBI's Executive Director. He will now lead the investigations department, leveraging his background in tackling fraud and market manipulation to ensure a fair playing field for investors.

title: Inside the F/A-18 Super Hornet: The backbone of the US Navy leading the air war in Iran
content: The F/A-18E/F Super Hornet remains a vital asset for the U.S. Navy, serving as the backbone of carrier air wings and a key component in "Operation Epic Fury" against Iranian military infrastructure. Despite the advent of stealth fighters, its reliability and cost-effectiveness ensure sustained strike capabilities well into the 2040s.

title: Summer travel to get costlier: Jet fuel prices jump amid Middle East conflict
content: Get ready for pricier flights as Middle East tensions escalate, driving up jet fuel costs significantly. Experts warn of inevitable fare hikes, especially on long-haul international routes, with some airlines already adjusting prices. Travelers are advised to book early and remain flexible to mitigate rising costs.

title: 'Never do what Bangladesh captain did': Ex-India star blasts Mehidy Hasan Miraz
content: Former India cricketer Mohammad Kaif strongly criticised Bangladesh captain Mehidy Hasan Miraz for a controversial run-out of Salman Ali Agha. Kaif deemed the unsportsmanlike act unacceptable, emphasizing that no wicket-taking desperation justifies such a dismissal, even in a high-stakes match.

title: 'Made to abort': Student rapes 3 minors, impregnates 1; detained
content: A 17-year-old student in Nagarkurnool was detained for allegedly raping and impregnating three minor girls, blackmailing them with filmed assaults. His friend, two doctors, and a nurse were arrested for illegally performing an abortion on one of the pregnant survivors. The accused faces multiple charges under Pocso and IT Acts.

title: Tata, Parle canteens feel LPG squeeze; India Inc redesigns menu — less tea, no samosas
content: India's industrial sector faces disruption as commercial LPG shortages impact factory operations. Manufacturers report production hurdles, particularly in brazing and paint shops, while canteens are paring back menus. Companies are turning to electric alternatives and even firewood to cope with the 'hand-to-mouth' supply situation, with government intervention underway to prioritize household needs.

title: Middle East conflict: India grants 30-day visa extension to stranded foreigners
content: India is extending visas for foreign nationals affected by the Middle East conflict. Overstay penalties will be waived for those delayed until February 28. Exit permits will be issued free of charge. Temporary Landing Permits are also being granted to diverted flights. This comes as the conflict escalates, impacting regional travel and energy supplies.

title: 'White House now begging India': Iran FM Araghchi mocks US U-turn on Russian oil
content: Iran's foreign minister Araghchi slammed the US for pressuring India to stop Russian oil imports, only to now "beg" for such purchases. He also criticized Europe's stance on the Ukraine conflict. Meanwhile, Iran assured safe passage for Indian vessels through the Strait of Hormuz, highlighting strong India-Iran ties and shared interests amidst regional tensions.

title: 'World’s ‘poorest president,' who lived in a farmhouse and donated 90 % of his salary
content: José Mujica, Uruguay's 'poorest president,' lived a life of profound humility, donating most of his salary and residing on a flower farm.  Despite a past as a guerrilla fighter and prisoner, he led his nation through progressive reforms, including legalizing marijuana and same-sex marriage. His legacy is one of integrity, proving leadership can thrive beyond opulent settings.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2 The Revenge' first review goes viral; Ranveer Singh's fans call out 'Fake reviews'
content: Anticipation for 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' is soaring, with alleged early reviews praising Ranveer Singh's performance. However, fans are questioning the authenticity of these unverified reports, as the film is yet to be certified. Director Aditya Dhar's wife, Yami Gautam, called the film 'beyond extraordinary,' hinting at a memorable cinematic experience.

title: Watch: Trump shares video of US bombing Iran's crucial oil hub Kharg Island
content: US President Donald Trump announced a major bombing raid on Iran's Kharg Island, sharing "unclassified" aerial footage. While claiming military targets were obliterated, Trump stated oil infrastructure was deliberately spared, warning of reconsideration if shipping through the Strait of Hormuz is threatened. Iran confirmed strikes but denied any infrastructure damage.

title: Ex-Australia wicketkeeper makes bold remark on Abhishek Sharma
content: Despite a shaky start with three consecutive ducks, Indian opener Abhishek Sharma proved his mettle in the T20 World Cup 2026 final. He smashed an explosive 18-ball fifty, setting the stage for India's dominant victory. Former Australian keeper Brad Haddin lauded Sharma's 'rascal' mentality, highlighting his ability to perform under pressure.

title: ‘Putin might be helping them’: Trump hints Russia could be aiding Iran
content: President Trump suggested Vladimir Putin might be aiding Iran in its conflict, drawing a parallel to US support for Ukraine. This comes after Trump previously questioned a reporter on similar reports.  Meanwhile, a US envoy stated Russia denied sharing intelligence with Iran, though expressed hope this remains true.

title: Who was Mumbai engineer Deonandan Prasad Singh, killed in tanker attack near Iraq?
content: A 54-year-old Indian seafarer from Mumbai has died after the oil tanker he was serving on was attacked near Iraq’s southern port city of Basra. Deonandan Prasad Singh sustained severe injuries during the incident and later succumbed to them. The tanker’s crew abandoned the vessel and were rescued. An investigation into the attack is underway.

title: 'When captain Hardik Pandya needs a shoulder, Rohit Sharma is there': Harbhajan Singh
content: Harbhajan Singh urges Mumbai Indians to keep Rohit Sharma actively on the field, not as an Impact Player. He believes Rohit's leadership experience can significantly aid captain Hardik Pandya in crucial match decisions. Rohit's strong performance last season, including surpassing 7,000 IPL runs, underscores his value.

title: Robbery for residency: How 11 Indians allegedly defrauded US authorities to claim U visas
content: Eleven Indian nationals face US charges for orchestrating fake armed robberies. The scheme aimed to allow store clerks to falsely claim victim status for immigration benefits.  Investigators revealed that 'victims' allegedly paid an organiser to participate, while store owners were compensated for allowing staged incidents.  This elaborate plot sought to exploit the U visa system.

title: Watch: Iran displays kamikaze naval drones in underground 'missile city'
content: Iran has revealed footage of its underground "missile cities" showcasing a vast arsenal of naval drones, anti-ship missiles, and sea mines. The nation claims to possess a significant stockpile of suicide drone boats capable of disrupting global trade through the Strait of Hormuz, a threat already demonstrated in regional conflicts.

title: Israel shares video claiming cluster bomb from Iranian missile fell inside empty school
content: Israel alleges Iran attempted to bomb a school, sharing security footage of cluster bombs. This follows a deadly strike on an Iranian school, which Iran claims killed over 165. US media reports the Iranian incident occurred during strikes near IRGC facilities. Tensions remain high after the reported killing of Iran's Supreme Leader.

title: NFL Trade Rumors: Nick Bosa’s San Francisco 49ers linked to $156M Buffalo Bills defensive end
content: The San Francisco 49ers are strongly linked to signing veteran defensive end Joey Bosa. He may play alongside his younger brother, Nick Bosa, who is also a defensive end. While Joey’s experience and talent are undeniable, his age and the 49ers’ 2026 strategy of preferring younger talent could become potential obstacles to a right priced deal. Else, the fans and analysts have always wanted to brothers play for one team.

title: Taylor Swift’s $2B news signals power future with Travis Kelce
content: Travis Kelce has confirmed he will return to the Kansas City Chiefs for another NFL season, signing a one year deal reportedly worth up to $15 million. The veteran tight end says his decision was driven by his continued love for football. Kelce also credited Taylor Swift’s passion for her craft as inspiration. With the couple expected to marry before training camp, both stars remain committed to thriving in their respective careers rather than slowing down.

title: WNBA: Urgent need to reach new CBA by Monday
content: WNBA commissioner Cathy Engelbert told reporters Friday that there's urgency to get a new collective bargaining agreement done by Monday to avoid disruptions to the preseason calendar, including training camp and preseason games.

title: U.S. tops Canada, advances to showdown vs. D.R.
content: The United States booked its spot in the WBC semifinals with a 5-3 victory against Canada on Friday night, joining the Dominican Republic team that mercy-ruled Korea in a 10-0 win in seven innings.

title: Bradley beats buzzer to put Arizona in Big 12 final
content: Jaden Bradley hit a fallaway jumper at the buzzer to send No. 2 Arizona to the Big 12 tournament finals with an 82-80 win over Iowa State on Friday night.

title: UCLA loses Bilodeau, takes out No. 8 Michigan St.
content: UCLA star Tyler Bilodeau suffered a right leg injury that will likely sideline him for at least one game, but the Bruins held off No. 8 Michigan State 88-84 to advance to the Big Ten tournament semifinals.

title: Leafs star Matthews (knee) to miss rest of season
content: Maple Leafs captain Auston Matthews has a torn MCL in his left knee and will miss the rest of the NHL season.

title: Warriors lose 4 more to injury as slide continues
content: Warriors coach Steve Kerr said his team is "as beaten up as any team I can ever remember" after losing four more players to injury Friday night, when they lost their fourth straight to fall two games behind the Clippers for the West's eighth seed.

title: Wilson returns as Thorns secure NWSL opener win
content: Sophia Wilson's return and Olivia Moultrie's winning strike highlighted the National Women's Soccer League season opener in the Portland Thorns' victory over the Washington Spirit on Friday night.

title: MAC commish predicts 2 bids after Miami (OH) loss
content: MAC commissioner Jon A. Steinbrecher is confident his conference will get two teams into the NCAA tournament for the first time in 27 years despite Miami (Ohio) losing to UMass in the league quarterfinals after going 31-0 in the regular season.

title: Men's Bracketology: The SEC teams that will sweat out Selection Sunday
content: Texas, Oklahoma and Auburn are all hanging around the cutline. Only the Sooners can currently control their destiny.

title: Men's Bubble Watch: UCLA graduates to a 'lock' with win over Michigan State
content: The Bruins held on to beat the Spartans in the Big Ten quarterfinals to solidify their NCAA tournament résumé.

title: Women's Bracketology: Bubble fate could boil down to Princeton and Ivy final
content: Richmond, Nebraska and Arizona State will breathe easier if Princeton wins the Ivy and keeps it a one-bid league.

title: Women's freshman impact rankings: The top 10 (plus 1) to end the regular season
content: Five players crack the final rankings, but none unseat Jazzy Davidson or Aaliyah Chavez from Nos. 1 and 2.

title: Where nine of the best remaining NFL free agents should sign: We found perfect landing spots
content: Where should Jauan Jennings, Joey Bosa and Rasheed Walker sign? Which roster needs Kirk Cousins? Let's find new homes for nine players still looking for one.

title: Live tracking NFL free agency: Updates on signings, trades, cuts
content: We're following NFL free agency signings, trades, cuts, contract restructures and other moves as they happen.

title: NHL playoff watch: Projections for the Oilers' postseason path
content: Here's how it's shaping up for Edmonton. Plus, full Stanley Cup playoff projections, draft lottery standings and more.

title: Weekend predictions: Can Spurs stop record losing streak at Liverpool?
content: It's Friday, and another full weekend of action awaits across the soccer calendar as Julien Laurens picks out the best games to watch in Europe.

title: NBA draft intel and latest buzz: Peterson or Dybantsa at No. 1? What's Boozer's case?
content: Here's what league insiders are saying about Darryn Peterson, AJ Dybantsa and more top NBA draft prospects.

title: Men's coaching tracker: Who could be up next at Cincinnati?
content: The Bearcats are moving on from Wes Miller after five seasons -- but have promising candidates on deck.

title: What Kyler Murray signing means for Vikings, J.J. McCarthy -- and Justin Jefferson's fantasy value
content: Will Murray resurrect his career and boost the Vikings' offense after being released by the Cardinals?

title: Full 2026 draft order: When teams will pick across all 257 selections, seven rounds
content: The order for all seven rounds of the 2026 NFL draft is set, starting with the Raiders at No. 1 and ending with the Broncos at No. 257.

title: 'Just wild': Bam Adebayo's 83-point performance resonated with his AAU coach
content: Ty White said his former high school star did something to wow him every game, but even this performance has him stunned.

title: NWSL 2026 mega-preview: Key moves, questions and predictions for all 16 teams
content: The NWSL is back! Let's predict how each team will finish by assessing what's ahead for Rodman, Lavelle and the other stars.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Chelsea winger Pedro Neto on Barcelona's radar
content: Barcelona are monitoring Chelsea's Pedro Neto in case they decide to strengthen at winger. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Hamilton: Ferrari losing 'a lot of time' to Mercedes
content: Lewis Hamilton has said Ferrari's gap to Mercedes is mainly due to a deficit in power unit performance after he qualified fourth for Saturday's Chinese Grand Prix sprint race.

title: Haney vs. Garcia 2? Mayweather vs. Tyson still happening?
content: Andreas Hale answers some key questions to bring clarity to the most intriguing storylines in boxing today.

title: Risers and fallers: Filipowski, da Silva moving up; Flagg a problem during playoffs?
content: Tristan da Silva is on the rise, and should fantasy basketball managers keep players like Bennedict Mathurin and Cameron Johnson?

title: Cheat sheet: Sleepers, busts, rookies and closers
content: You've read their names all preseason long, it'd be a shame to forget them on draft day. The ESPN Cheat Sheet is one way to make sure that doesn't happen.

title: Guide to NFL offseason signings: Can Geno Smith rebound from 2025 in New York?
content: Your one-stop shop for fantasy football reaction to all the key player movement via free agency and trades.

title: India Announces 30-day Visa Extension To Foreigners In Country Amid Conflict
content: India has announced the extension of visas and waiver of overstay penalty to foreigners stranded in the country due to the ongoing conflict in Middle East.

title: 4 Rounds Of Phone Calls, PM's Outreach
content: Two India-flagged Shipping Corporation of India (SCI) LPG tankers have crossed the Strait of Hormuz today and are headed towards India.

title: Climate Activist Sonam Wangchuk, Detained For Nearly 6 Months, To Be Released
content: Sonam Wangchuk was detained on September 26 last year, two days after violent protests over statehood and constitutional protection left four people dead and 50, including security personnel, injured in Ladakh's Leh.

title: North Korea Fires Ballistic Missile Towards Sea During US-South Korea Drills
content: The projectile was fired toward the sea off North Korea's east coast, the South Korean military said in a brief message to reporters. It gave no further details. Japan's coast guard said the projectile appeared to have fallen into the sea.

title: "Orphan Pearl" On Fire: The Critical Oil Island Struck By US In Iran
content: Spread over a meagre 20 square kilometres, Iran's Kharg island processes 90 per cent of the country's total oil exports, driving the region to the edge as the US attacked it on Friday, 15 days into the Middle East conflict.

title: '$150, Hope': US Senate Pays Tribute To Indian-American Businessman Sunil Puri
content: The US Senate paid tribute to Indian-American businessman and philanthropist Sunil Puri, with Senator Dick Durbin recalling his journey from a young immigrant arriving from India with little money to a civic leader who helped transform Rockford.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Trump And His America Are Fast Losing Touch With Reality
content: What is the guiding logic of the rather brash turn in American foreign policy? It is all too easy to box Trump as an irrational player, but frankly, it is a bit of an analytical copout.

title: India Exports First GI-Tagged Joha Rice Consignment From Assam To UK, Italy
content: The Agricultural and Processed Food Products Export Development Authority (APEDA) has facilitated the first export consignment of 25 metric tonnes of GI-tagged Joha rice from Assam to the United Kingdom and Italy, the Commerce Ministry said.

title: Want To Renounce US Citizenship? Fee Now Slashed By 80% To $450
content: After years of legal battles with several groups representing Americans wanting to give up their citizenship, the department on Friday published a final rule in the Federal Register that reduces the cost from $2,350 to $450.

title: Man Who Rammed Into Michigan Synagogue Had Lost Family In An Israeli Strike
content: A Lebanese-born man who had learned a week earlier that four of his family members were killed in an Israeli airstrike in his native country, waited in his car outside a synagogue for two hours before ramming into the building.

title: 2 Men Kill Friend In Mumbai After Fish 'Disappears' From Pan. Rats Had Eaten It
content: According to the police, the incident took place on February 24 when the victim, identified as Afsar, and his two friends - Suresh and Rajesh Rameshnath alias Nepali - had cooked fish in their house.

title: US-Israel-Iran War LIVE: Trump Says Iran Wants A Deal, But "Not The One That I Would Accept"
content: US-Israel-Iran War News LIVE Updates: Buildings in Dubai shook after loud explosions were heard, and a thick cloud of smoke rose over a central part of the city.

title: Spelling Error Reveals Teachers' Recruitment Scam In Rajasthan, 20 Arrested
content: The Rajasthan Police has arrested 20 people including 19 candidates who secured employment under the "Outstanding Athlete" quota in the third-grade teachers recruitment-2022 using fake Taekwondo certificates.

title: 'China Owner', Shadow Fleet - The Passkeys To Cross Strait Of Hormuz
content: Supply chains remain disrupted, commodity prices have shot up and oil prices have crossed $100 a barrel as a war rages in the Middle East, particularly disrupting movement in the strategically critical Strait of Hormuz.

title: Delhi Police Cautions People Against Cyber Fraud Amid LPG Shortage
content: The Delhi Police has cautioned citizens against clicking on suspicious links or trusting calls from unknown numbers claiming to be from gas agencies, warning that cyber fraudsters may exploit the ongoing panic over LPG availability.

title: "Strong Odour" Halts Flight Ops At 4 Major US Airports
content: Four airports serving Washington, DC, Baltimore and Richmond, Virginia, halted all flights on Friday evening for over an hour because of a strong chemical smell that was impeding air traffic controllers.

title: Bhopal Declared Water-Scarce Zone, New Private Borewells Banned
content: In a proactive measure to combat rapidly declining groundwater levels, Bhopal Collector Kaushalendra Vikram Singh has declared the entire district a "water-scarce zone" and imposed an immediate ban on the digging of new private tube wells.

title: Iranian Leaders Trying To Prove Power Of Khamenei's Clerical-Based System
content: Iran's leaders are seeking to show the staying power of their near half-century old clerical-based system and prove that it can withstand the killing of its longtime ruler and war with United States and Israel, analysts said.

title: "Patently Ridiculous": Trump Administration Lashes Out At CNN Over Iran War
content: The Pentagon and White House slammed CNN over its Iran war coverage Friday -- with Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth saying he looked forward to an ally of President Donald Trump taking over the news network.

title: Summer Flights Likely To Get Expensive As Jet Fuel Prices Rise Amid Iran War
content: Jet fuel prices are rising as the war in the Middle East disrupts global oil supplies, putting cost pressure on airlines as the busy summer travel season approaches.

title: LPG shortage sparks surge in firewood demand in Kerala
content: Hotels and eateries turn to alternative fuel as commercial cylinder supply dries up; sawmills and plywood units report rising inquiries amid price hike

title: Karnataka High Court upholds KPTCL regulations entrusting departmental inquiries to Lokayukta
content: The single judge had held that conduct of both the investigation and the departmental inquiry by the officers of the Lokayukta amounts to “inevitable bias” against the employee and offends Article 14 of the Constitution of India

title: Centre revokes detention of climate activist Sonam Wangchuk under NSA
content: Government has decided to revoke the detention of Sonam Wangchuk with immediate effect by exercising the powers available under the National Security Act

title: Case against school after children being allegedly forced to stand in blazing sun in Yelahanka
content: The incident reportedly occurred at a school in Attur Layout, where parents alleged that young children were forced to remain standing outside in the blazing sun as punishment

title: Nine-judge SC Bench to hear arguments on March 17 on definition of 'industry'
content: A seven-judge Constitution Bench headed by then CJI T.S. Thakur in 2017 said it was of the opinion that the appeals before it be placed before a Bench comprising nine judges keeping in view the "serious and wide-ranging implications" of the issue

title: Telangana Civil Supplies Minister to meet with LPG Distributors Association on March 14
content: People were urged to dial toll free number 1967 to report hoarding or lodge grievances pertaining to the supply

title: Lt Gen (Retd) Syed Ata Hasnain sworn in as Bihar Governor
content: The ceremony was attended by Chief Minister Nitish Kumar, his cabinet colleagues and senior officers, among others

title: Several Maharashtra Cities Record Temperatures Above 40°C; Rain and Thunderstorms Predicted from March 17
content: Regions including Vidarbha, Marathwada, Khandesh, and parts of Central Maharashtra are expected to be affected by rain, thunderstorms, and gusty winds between March 17 and 20

title: Petrol, diesel, and domestic LPG supply in the State remains normal, says Haryana Civil Supplies Minister
content: “Hundreds of thousands of cylinders were being distributed daily across the State through a network of over 5,000 distributors,” says Minister

title: Debut in politics, Nishant Kumar remains busy with party activities
content: Junior Mr. Kumar who is likely to become one of the new Deputy Chief Ministers of the State is visiting Temples, Gurudwara, Mazar and various other locations to pay homage to eminent leaders to seek their blessings after joining the party

title: Bhopal gas tragedy: Madhya Pradesh HC asks government to share clean-up plan
content: In an action taken report, the government told the court that a meeting chaired by Additional Chief Secretary Ashok Barnwal was held on March 3 to discuss a time-bound remediation of the Union Carbide India Ltd. (UCIL) factory

title: Rahul Gandhi pays tribute to Kanshi Ram ahead of birth anniversary, vows to fight for marginalised
content: “Today we remember Kanshi Ram ji’s struggle, his vision, and his influence on Indian politics. I thank him wholeheartedly. We pledge to follow his path and fight for the issues of marginalised sections. If the Congress had functioned effectively at that time, Kanshi Ram would not have needed to wage a separate struggle,” Rahul Gandhi

title: Attack on Manipur woman: NHRC issues notice to Delhi Police
content: Police apprehended four minors for the attack, in which she suffered injuries to her face, and for hurling racial and sexual slurs at her

title: 11 Indian nationals charged in U.S. over visa fraud scheme
content: The charge of conspiracy to commit visa fraud provides for a sentence of up to five years in prison, three years of supervised release and a fine of $250,000

title: Pi Day 2026: significance of the mathematical constant π
content: March 14 is celebrated globally as Pi Day as the first digits of the mathematical constant π, 3.14159, match the date 3/14

title: The curious case of SCR’s twin stations
content: From Lallaguda to Ammuguda, South Central Railway hosts rare “twin stations” sharing identical names yet serving different roles and routes. While some function purely as operational signalling points, others handle passengers, creating unique railway geography and occasional commuter confusion

title: What are the changes being proposed to Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Act, 2019 | Explained
content: How is the government proposing to alter the definition of “transgender person”? Why has it argued for the omission of the right to “self-perceived gender identity”? What about the proposed changes have raised concerns among the trans community? What has the Supreme Court laid down in the landmark 2014 judgement recognising a third gender beyond male and female and the right to self-determination of gender identity?

title: India roots for benefits system at pandemic agreement talks
content: India has found common cause with a coalition of developing countries that says developing countries that share pathogen materials and genetic sequence data must receive fair, concrete, and legally enforceable benefits in return

title: CM Mamata announces development boards for marginalised communities ahead of Bengal Assembly polls
content: The announcement comes days after President Droupadi Murmu stated that the West Bengal government was not serious about development of Scheduled Tribes in the State

title: Restored Kamarajar Salai in Arcot town opened for public use
content: Municipal officials said that a key feature of the restoration of the stretch was an increase in its total height by two feet to the existing level of stormwater drains; this is to avoid inundation

title: New District Munsif and Judicial Magistrate Court near Katpadi inaugurated
content: Officials of the Public Works Department, which completed the work, said that the new court will function inside the refurbished panchayat office premises in the village

title: Udhayanidhi Stalin to inaugurate lounge for construction workers, village knowledge centre in Tirupattur
content: Spread over 350 sq.ft, the all-weather lounge is fitted with fans, benches, mobile phone charging units, and drinking water facilities

title: Congregational prayers, rallies held in Kashmir on Ramzan’s last Friday; Jamia Masjid closed
content: The Mirwaiz said it’s for the seventh consecutive year that the rulers have denied Muslims the right to offer prayers at Jama Masjid on this auspicious day

title: Seven dead, 92 inmates infected with herpes virus at overcrowded Jalpaiguri correctional home
content: About 92 inmates have been infected in the outbreak of the Herpes Simplex Virus between August 20, 2025 and March 9, 2026. Seven persons infected by the virus have passed away.  The number of inmates at correctional home with a capacity 700 is over 1200

title: West Asia crisis: The Indians caught in the crossfire
content: Since February 28, when Israel and the U.S. jointly attacked Iran, plunging the entire West Asian region into war, thousands of Indian tourists and expatriates have been left stranded across the Gulf. Indian missions have struggled to keep up with requests and demands. From missile and drone strikes, to soaring prices, to communication blackouts, the conflict has left civilians vulnerable, reports Kallol Bhattacherjee

title: Retention rate below 50% in BMC schools, finds report
content: According to the report, titled ‘State of Municipal Education in Mumbai’, during the period, the transition of students from Class 8 to Class 9 declined by 30%, and only 80% of students moved from Class 9 to Class 10

title: Exercise LAMITIYE-2026: India and Seychelles carry out joint military exercise
content: A series of professional interactions, tactical discussions and practical drills have been conducted as part of the exercise. Operational demonstrations have been a key component of LAMITIYE-2026.

title: IndiGo to levy fuel charge for domestic and international flights from March 14
content: IndiGo said it would continue to monitor the situation and make relevant adjustments as and when appropriate.

title: Opposition leaders press government, Lok Sabha Speaker to revoke the suspension of 8 MPs
content: The eight MPs, including seven from the Congress, have been barred from attending the remainder of the Budget Session

title: Maharashtra govt. tables Bill to ban ‘unlawful conversions’
content: It proposes stringent provisions for conversions carried out through coercion, fraud, inducement, or marriage; objective to protect right to freedom of religion, says Minister while introducing Bill

title: Futuristic mindfulness city fuels Bhutan’s ‘fly east’ via Assam
content: Drukair says the commencement of Paro-Singapore flight via Guwahati makes commercial sense because of altitude-dictated weight restrictions that force aeroplanes to take off from or land at Paro Airport with less fuel

title: Chennai Corporation launches projects, expedites works ahead of poll
content: Greater Chennai Corporation (GCC) has expedited civic infrastructure projects, including work on 17 bridges, in various zones of the city

title: Stalin announces pension, family pension hike for journalists
content: M.K.Stalin also directed officials to take steps to provide medical insurance under the Chief Minister’s Comprehensive Health Insurance Scheme to all journalists holding accredited identity cards

title: Kerala Cabinet approves Film Policy
content: It was prepared after discussions with stakeholders in the film industry, ideas shared at the Film Conclave and opinions gathered from the public

title: VCK hopes to keep its ‘recognised party’ tag
content: Party leaders also feel a higher allotment of seats would justify its consistent support to the DMK front since 2017

title: Nitish Kumar hints at Samrat Choudhary as his successor in Bihar
content: During the ongoing Samriddhi Yatra in Saharsa, the Chief Minister placed his hands on Mr. Choudhary’s shoulders and urged the public to show their support to the Deputy CM

title: Fuel crisis: hoteliers advised to trim menus, cut operating hours
content: Finance Minister suggests alternate day operations as hotel bodies seek at least 50% quota of commercial LPG cylinders to sustain operations in the State

title: Kerala Cabinet approves formulation of land assignment rules for use of pattaya land for other purposes
content: Fees will be waived for houses and for buildings constructed for agricultural and educational purposes, as well as markets, places of worship, structures for public purposes, cultural institutions, clubs and structures for charitable establishments

title: Haryana Rajya Sabha polls: Congress MLAs reach Himachal Pradesh to prevent cross-voting
content: After CLP meet, about 30 MLAs leave for Shimla; party insiders say the move is meant to keep the flock together ahead of three-cornered election; Congress holds 37 seats and needs 31 votes to send its candidate

title: 27 Indian-flagged ships remain stranded in Persian Gulf, one starts moving: Government official
content: Jag Prakash, an oil tanker with a carrying capacity of 48,000 tonnes, is headed to Tanzania, the official says; three cargo ships with 76 seafarers aboard are located in the Gulf of Oman, while 24 ships are stranded in the Persian Gulf; there are 23,000 Indian nationals working in the region on merchant ships, ports and offshore vessels

title: How many constituencies can a candidate contest from? | TN Elections with The Hindu
content:
In the 2001 Tamil Nadu Assembly elections, AIADMK supremo Jayalalithaa filed nominations from four seats and 
all four nominations were rejected. Protests erupted. The ruling government was accused of conspiracy.
And ten years later, the Supreme Court pulled up that leader sharply. That leader was AIADMK supremo Jayalalithaa. How many constituencies can a candidate legally contest from in India?

title: In Rajya Sabha, BJP MP proposes setting up National Commission for persons with disabilities
content: MP Bhim Singh said that the architecture of such a National Commission should be similar to other National Commissions, have “independent powers” and have regional offices to ensure accessibility.

title: Collector reviews LPG stocks at Kondapalli bottling plant
content: Lakshmisha assures public there is no shortage as oil companies can supply over 25,000 cylinders daily against demand of 16,000 in Vijayawada; warns of action against black-marketing and rumour-mongering

title: PM distributes land deeds to Assam’s tea workers
content: In the poll-bound State, PM Narendra Modi says plantation workers had to wait years for land rights because Congress never cared for them during their tenure; this is the first such initiative in 200 years of Assam tea

title: Supreme Court declines plea to make nucleic acid tests compulsory at blood banks
content: NAT tests are more expensive but more accurate in detecting HIV, Hepatitis B, C viruses in the blood; petitioner sought to protect thalassemia patients who require frequent blood transfusions and are at risk from infected blood

title: IPL 2026 in Bengaluru likely to get nod after stadium safety overhaul
content: Home Minister G. Parameshwara is likely to announce the clearance on Monday (March 16) after a meeting with the committee where the final report will be submitted.

title: Kerala HC directs to defer nurses’ strike till March 19, suggests mediation
content: Stating that any continuing difference of opinion between the nurses and private hospital managements will affect the people seeking treatment and other members of the public, the court says that it will be ideal if both sides choose mediation

title: Bengaluru shows consistently low PM2.5 levels compared to other cities, says report
content: Seasonal mean PM2.5 concentrations in Bengaluru indicate comparatively better air quality than northern cities, with clear seasonal variability across 2024 and 2025

title: Tracing Hyderabad’s centuries-old Persian connections
content: From the dargah of Ibne Khatoon to Irani cafes and Muharram majlis, Hyderabad’s mourning for Iran’s leader reflects centuries-old cultural connections forged by Persian migrants, Qutb Shahi rulers, diplomats, and traders who transformed the Deccan’s language, food, and traditions

title: Focus is on modernising agriculture, improving farmers’ income, says Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu
content: An amount of ₹2,670 crore has been credited to the accounts of 46.85 lakh farmers towards third instalment of the PM Kisan-Annadata Sukhibhava programme, and this includes the State’s share of ₹1,874 crore

title: NHRC issues notices to five States over rise in missing persons cases
content: The Commission takes cognisance of media reports indicating 12,000 and 14,000 missing persons cases annually since 2013 in Bihar alone; observes the issue raises serious concerns of human rights violations

title: Cong. MLA B.R. Patil, another accused of committing irregularities in development works
content: Subhash Guttedar says that crores of rupees have been misappropriated through poor execution of road works in Aland constituency

title: Thiruvananthapuram Collector’s report contradicts Corporation’s claim on Attukal Pongala cleanup
content: While the Mayor had said that cleaning was completed within a few hours of the festival, the Collector’s report points out that the waste removal was completed only within three days

title: Kerala government decides to lift construction ban in Idukki
content: Title deed holders can now build homes and utilise their land. Individuals can construct up to 5,000 sq ft without any fees, while commercial projects will incur charges based on land fair value. Activist express concerns that the timing may be politically motivated ahead of upcoming Assembly elections.

title: Congress stages protest outside ITC factory near Yelwal over tobacco price crash
content: Karnataka Pradesh Congress Committee spokesperson M. Lakshman says tobacco prices, which had dropped to around ₹240 per kg after companies scaled down procurement, showed signs of recovery on March 13.

title: Kerala Assembly Polls 2026 | Sudhakaran hints at ‘revolt’ as Congress decides to keep MPs off Assembly polls
content: Sudhakaran’s ‘hasty return’ from Delhi to Kannur, when Parliament is in session, seems to have rocked the Congress’s bid to present a united face at the hustings. He asserted last week that he would ‘100 per cent” contest the polls from Kannur

title: Latest mini NFL mock draft: Yates projects picks 1-10 based on the free agency frenzy
content: trades, re-signings and big-name trades have affected how 2026 draft might unfold . some teams are slated to pick in the top 10 of April's first round . watch this as we start to get a clearer picture of what might happen on April 23 .

title: Olympic champ Alysa Liu receives key to city in hometown rally
content: alysa Liu won the first winter games gold medal by an american woman in more than two decades . she won gold in the women's singles competition and again as part of the u.s. team event . mayor Barbara Lee gifted her the key at the rally honoring the gold medalist .

title: Can the Knicks get Towns, Brunson on track? Five issues they need to solve -- fast
content: the new york Knicks coach speaks of a standard, not unlike former Pittsburgh Steelers coach Mike Tomlin's . "the standard is the standard," he says in his news conferences . the coach was hired last July under the expectation that he would elevate the Knick to play until June .

title: Inside Man United's big decisions between now and next season, from manager to transfers
content: manchester united are in pole position to secure champions league qualification . united have climbed to third place in the premier league after 19 points from 24 . despite the upturn in fortunes since carrick's arrival, uncertainty remains over his future .

title: Saints' Etienne corrects how to say his name
content: RB Travis Etienne is setting the record straight on his last name . Etienne said his name was pronounced like "Achane" growing up . he said he gave up trying to change it and accepted the alternate pronunciation .
